testBaseline.note: Ruff errors are pre-existing in src/server/ai/ollama.py and tests/test_remote_ollama.py — not sprint scope. Baseline from Sprint 7 end (9/9 stories, 1720 passed + 11 from Ralph's I-011/I-012 work if completed before sprint start).

stories.title: API key authentication middleware
stories.intent: Secure all server endpoints (except /health) so only authorized devices can push data. This is a prerequisite for the sync and analyze endpoints.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
src/server/api/auth.py implements a FastAPI dependency that extracts X-API-Key header
API key compared against API_KEY from Settings using hmac.compare_digest() for constant-time comparison
Missing key returns 401 with {"detail": "Missing API key"}
Invalid key returns 401 with {"detail": "Invalid API key"}
GET /api/v1/health is exempt from auth (no dependency injected)
All other endpoints require the auth dependency
invariants: GET /api/v1/health still returns 200 without any API key
No changes to src/pi/ or src/common/
stopConditions: If Settings.API_KEY is not available from config, STOP and file BL-

stories.title: Delta sync endpoint
stories.intent: Accept delta data from the Pi (or any authorized device). This is the walk phase's primary data pipeline — replaces the crawl phase's manual load_data.py import with a real HTTP endpoint.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
POST /api/v1/sync accepts JSON body: {deviceId, batchId, tables: {tableName: {lastSyncedId, rows: [...]}}}
Pydantic request model validates: deviceId required (non-empty), batchId required, tables required
Accepted table names: realtime_data, statistics, profiles, vehicle_info, ai_recommendations, connection_log, alert_log, calibration_sessions — any other table name rejected with 422
Upserts rows using INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE with key (source_device, source_id)
Pi id field maps to source_id; MariaDB generates its own id (autoincrement PK)
Server sets synced_at=NOW(), source_device=request.deviceId, sync_batch_id=request.batchId on each row
Single transaction: all table upserts in one MariaDB transaction; on any error, entire batch rolls back
Creates sync_history record with batch_id, device_id, started_at, completed_at, tables_synced, rows_inserted, rows_updated, status
driveDataReceived is true if connection_log rows contain event_type=drive_end
Max payload size 10MB (configurable via MAX_SYNC_PAYLOAD_MB); returns 413 if exceeded
On MariaDB error: returns 500, sync_history status=failed
invariants: Idempotent: syncing same batchId twice produces same MariaDB state (updated count, not duplicate inserts)
Transaction safety: partial failure rolls back entire batch
stopConditions: If async SQLAlchemy upsert syntax for MariaDB is unclear, STOP and propose sync vs async approach

stories.title: Stub AI analysis endpoint
stories.intent: Minimal stub for debugging the Pi-to-Server-to-AI loop. Returns a canned response with the correct shape so US-CMP-005 (run phase) can slot in without Pi-side changes. Writes to analysis_history for inspection.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
POST /api/v1/analyze accepts JSON body with drive_id and parameters fields
Returns 200 with JSON: {status: "ok", analysis_id: "stub-{uuid}", message: "Stub analysis — real implementation pending US-CMP-005", recommendations: [], model: "stub", processingTimeMs: 0}
Response contract matches expected shape from Spool's tuning spec (so US-CMP-005 can slot in without Pi-side changes)
Writes request to analysis_history table with analysis_id, model=stub, processing_time_ms=0, status=completed
API key authentication required (uses US-CMP-002 middleware)
Missing auth returns 401, malformed body returns 422
invariants: No changes to src/server/analytics/ (analytics engine is separate from AI endpoint)
Response shape must be forward-compatible with US-CMP-005 real implementation

stories.title: Sync-to-analytics parity validation
stories.intent: Integration test proving analytics produce identical results regardless of whether data arrived via bulk import (crawl path) or HTTP sync (walk path). This validates the walk phase is production-ready before moving to run phase with real data.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
Integration test seeds same simulator scenario data via two paths: bulk import (load_data.py logic) and HTTP sync (POST /api/v1/sync)
Runs basic analytics (computeDriveStatistics, compareDriveToHistory) on both data sets
Asserts per-drive statistics (min, max, avg, std) match within 0.01% tolerance
Asserts drive_summary rows match between crawl and walk paths
Asserts drive_statistics rows match between crawl and walk paths
Asserts anomaly detection produces same flagged parameters
Test marked @pytest.mark.integration and @pytest.mark.parity
invariants: No modifications to any src/ files — this is a test-only story
Architecture constraint validated: no analytics code depends on data source
stopConditions: If sync endpoint (US-CMP-004) is not yet passing, this story is BLOCKED
